mission.title: jaeger: Allow configuration of jaeger client in the query service
mission.description: Allow configuration of jaeger client in the query service. Requested by 8+ users.

steps.title: Check current jaeger deployment
steps.description:
Verify your jaeger version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
helm list -n jaeger 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working jaeger installation.

steps.title: Review jaeger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant jaeger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get configmap -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=jaeger
```
## Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?
The jaeger query service allows for tracing the query service itself.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow configuration of jaeger client in the query service
steps.description:
## Which problem is this PR solving?
The query service shall now be configured using the environmental variables to configure the tracer.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get events -n jaeger --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow configuration of jaeger client in the query service" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Which problem is this PR solving?
The query service shall now be configured using the environmental variables to configure the tracer.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with jaeger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
